Revelation 22:10
The Prophecies

Six Anchors in Scripture and History

Each prophecy is a precision instrument — verified by independent archaeology, secular chronology, and the fulfilment of centuries.

Daniel's 70 Weeks

Predicted the exact year of Christ's baptism — 500 years in advance.

Dan 9:24–27
→

The 1,260 Years

Six Bible texts. One period. 538–1798 AD — verified to the decade.

Dan 7:25 · Rev 12:6
→

The Second Coming

Not two events. One visible, audible, glorious return — every eye sees Him.

Rev 1:7 · 1 Thess 4:16
→

The Antichrist

Identified from Scripture alone by Luther, Calvin, and every Reformer.

Dan 7:8 · 2 Thess 2:3
→

Daniel 2 — The Image

Four kingdoms, then God's. History confirmed every limb of the statue.

Dan 2:31–45
→

The Mark of the Beast

Identified in history. Not a chip — a mark of allegiance in worship.

Rev 13:16–17
→

The Reformation Consensus

Luther, Calvin, Newton, and Wesley All Agreed

The Historicist reading of Daniel and Revelation was the dominant Protestant position for 300 years — a legacy of rigorous scholarship and Christ-centred clarity.
